my_thoughts: 'But I don't know what to look for'—they accepted I won't judge (good) but need guidance (reasonable). The internal markers ('more or less anxious? more or less alive?') give them concrete felt-sense tools. Not 'am I doing it right?' (external) but 'how do I feel?' (internal). This teaches them to use body/affect as progress indicator. Anxiety down = progress. Aliveness up = progress. Simple, internal, non-judgmental. Two turns from 'tell me if I'm making progress' to 'check your own felt experience.' Clean handoff of authority from teacher to student.
accumulated_insight: T2 (Final): Student protests: 'But I don't know what to look for'—request_clarification shows they accepted teacher won't judge but need guidance on self-assessment. Teacher provides internal markers: 'Do you feel more or less anxious? More or less alive?' Shifts from abstract 'progress' to concrete felt experience. Two-turn arc: external validation seeking -> authority refused -> request for method -> internal markers given. Teaching point: progress is felt, not measured externally. Student now has tools for self-assessment without teacher judgment.
visible_response:
Not really. Since I haven’t heard anything from you, I have no idea whether you have an internal emotional problem or not.
Only you can know for sure.
